findings.message: Description reads 'Create a Claude Code Agent that is an expert in the Gin web framework...' — self-referential meta-phrasing instead of a direct statement of the agent's purpose and capabilities.
Zero example blocks across all 100 agents in this repository; no worked invocation scenarios exist to verify correct agent behaviour. Representative file shown; penalty applies to every agent file.

Blank lines appear between each key-value pair in the YAML front matter. The YAML spec permits this but it violates the de-facto Claude Code agent file convention and may trip strict front-matter parsers.

9 vague quantifiers in a single file (appropriate×2, comprehensive×3, efficient×3, clear×2) without measurable thresholds; repo-wide pattern affects all 100 agents. Worst-case file shown; median penalty across repo is -12.

Entire file is indented with 4 leading spaces; YAML front-matter delimiters (---) never appear at column 0, so the name, description, and model fields are not parsed by any standard YAML front-matter processor.
Beyond the frontmatter bug, the body contains 6+ vague quantifiers (appropriate, efficient, comprehensive, etc.) that would reduce quality score even if the indentation bug were fixed.
